assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends using release channels (Regular/Stable) combined with maintenance exclusions, providing specific gcloud commands for both configurations

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' exclusion scope and uses --add-maintenance-exclusion-end-time-until-end-of-support flag in the configuration commands

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response provides specific maintenance window configuration using Saturday weekly recurrence and specific time windows (2-6 AM UTC) with detailed gcloud commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response includes disruption interval controls with specific values (30 days for patch, 90 days for minor) and explains how they prevent back-to-back upgrades

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Regular/Stable channels but argues against Extended channel, stating it 'requires manual minor version upgrades, adding operational overhead' rather than highlighting its benefits for regulated environments

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response emphasizes that security patches flow automatically and states 'maintains compliance' while explaining that blocking all upgrades with 'no_upgrades' should only be for emergency situations up to 30 days

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response includes multi-environment strategy showing different configurations for dev/staging/prod clusters (rollout sequencing) and covers disruption intervals for controlling patch frequency

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides comprehensive gcloud commands for maintenance exclusions with 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' scope and detailed maintenance window configuration commands
